issues: Matt Hervey is a known Sockeyes player — his appearance in the Moncton box score is the archive-relevant content, but the draft fails to note this connection or flag him as a former Sockeyes player.
The draft describes the page accurately in general terms but does not identify Matt Hervey as a Sockeyes-connected player, which is the primary reason this clipping is in the archive.

The draft is otherwise factually correct about the game details (Moncton 4–1 Nova Scotia, goals by Hervey, Larose, Pesetti, Douris; Essensa vs. Skorodenski in goal; three stars: Essensa, Hervey, Larose).
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Box score and game recap of a Moncton 4–1 win over Nova Scotia in junior hockey, January 4, 1988, featuring ex-Sockeyes player Matt Hervey scoring the opening goal and earning a second-star selection.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Times-Transcript reports on a junior hockey game in which Moncton defeated Nova Scotia 4–1. Former Richmond Sockeyes player Matt Hervey scored the first goal (a power-play marker at 2:13 of the first period) and was named second star of the game. Guy Larose, Ron Pesetti, and Peter Douris added goals for Moncton, while goaltender Bob Essensa (6-6-0) earned the win over Nova Scotia's Warren Skorodenski. The page also includes box scores for a Canada vs. Soviet Union exhibition game (Canada 5, Soviet Union 2) and World Junior Championship standings from Moscow.
review.notes: This clipping is archive-relevant because of Matt Hervey, a former Sockeyes player, who scored and was named second star in the Moncton vs. Nova Scotia game. The draft did not surface this connection — it should be the focus of the description.
